name: LLM Agents for Scientific Discovery
description: Autonomous LLM-based agents performing literature review, hypothesis generation, and experiment design in scientific workflows.
why: Explosive growth driven by GPT-4/Claude capabilities, massive industry investment, and early success in chemistry and biology.

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) at Scale
description: Scaling RAG systems beyond simple vector lookup to include hierarchical retrieval, multi-hop reasoning, and citation grounding.
why: RAG has become the default production pattern; research focus shifting to quality, hallucination reduction, and domain-specific optimization.

seed_papers.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique

name: Efficient Fine-Tuning and Adaptation
description: Parameter-efficient methods (LoRA, QLoRA, adapters) for specializing foundation models with minimal compute and data.
why: Democratization of model customization drives practical interest; competition is fierce but open problems remain in multi-task and continual learning.

seed_papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

name: Multimodal Foundation Models
description: Unified architectures processing text, images, video, and structured data within single models.
why: GPT-4V, Gemini, and open alternatives have opened a new capability frontier; evaluation methodology is still immature.

name: Code Generation and Program Synthesis
description: LLM-driven code generation with verification, test synthesis, and repository-level understanding.
why: Industry adoption accelerating (Copilot, Cursor); academic focus on correctness guarantees and benchmark saturation.

seed_papers.title: SWE-bench: Evaluating LLMs on Real-World Software Engineering

name: Alignment and Safety
description: Constitutional AI, RLHF alternatives, red-teaming frameworks, and scalable oversight for frontier models.
why: Regulatory pressure and scaling concerns make this a high-priority area with strong funding but immature evaluation.

seed_papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback

name: Graph Neural Networks for Molecular Design
description: GNN-based approaches for drug discovery, material science, and protein structure prediction.
why: AlphaFold's success created sustained interest; competition with diffusion-based methods is the current frontier.

name: Federated and Privacy-Preserving ML
description: Training and inference under privacy constraints using federated learning, differential privacy, and secure computation.
why: Regulatory drivers (GDPR, HIPAA) sustain interest; practical deployment challenges provide ample research directions.

seed_papers.title: Advances in Federated Learning: A Comprehensive Survey

name: Diffusion Models Beyond Images
description: Applying diffusion model principles to 3D generation, video synthesis, audio, and structured data.
why: Image generation is mature; extension to other modalities offers novel contributions with established theoretical foundations.

name: Causal Inference with Machine Learning
description: Combining causal reasoning with deep learning for treatment effect estimation, counterfactual prediction, and causal discovery.
why: Growing recognition that correlation-based ML is insufficient for decision-making; methodological gaps remain in high-dimensional settings.

name: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
description: RLHF variants, DPO, and reward model training for aligning language models with human preferences.
why: Core training paradigm for frontier models; theoretical understanding lags behind practice, creating research opportunities.

name: Autonomous Driving Perception
description: End-to-end perception, BEV representation, occupancy prediction, and world models for self-driving vehicles.
why: Industry investment remains strong; transition from modular to end-to-end creates benchmark and evaluation opportunities.
